course.title: Huawei Cloud Service Certification Training HCIA-Cloud Service Lab Guide

sections.title: Introduction & Configuration Roadmap
learning_objectives: Understand HUAWEI CLOUD accounts, subaccounts, and related configurations
Learn how to register a HUAWEI CLOUD account
Learn how to purchase and release HUAWEI CLOUD resources
real_world_scenarios: Initial setup of a cloud ecosystem for an enterprise or individual developer using the official roadmap

sections.title: Account Registration and Real-Name Authentication
learning_objectives: Register a valid HUAWEI ID
Navigate the user console to access account settings
Complete individual real-name authentication processes
key_concepts: Real-name authentication enforcement policy for purchasing resources

definitions.definition: The master account credentials used to manage resources and financial billing on the HUAWEI CLOUD platform.

comparisons.individual_authentication: Designed for private persons; requires personal certificates/identification metadata.
comparisons.enterprise_authentication: Designed for legal corporate entities; requires corporate registration documentation.

limitations: Resource purchasing is restricted until real-name authentication is completed successfully.
exam_traps: Believing resources can be provisioned immediately after registration without doing real-name verification first.
common_confusions: Confusing the general HUAWEI ID master account with an IAM sub-user login option.
real_world_scenarios: An engineer initializing a fresh corporate cloud tenancy under regulatory compliance mandates.

sections.title: Creating an IAM User and Assigning Permissions
learning_objectives: Create a User Group and assign specific system-defined roles
Create an IAM user with management console access type
Log in via the subaccount interface and verify permissions scope
key_concepts: Centralized financial billing under the master account

definitions.definition: A user created by a HUAWEI CLOUD account within Identity and Access Management who uses cloud services and possesses independent identity credentials.
A collection of IAM users utilized as the administrative unit to simplify resource authorization and access controls.

numbers.context: Duration required for newly selected permissions to fully take effect across the user group.

comparisons.master_account: Charged and pays independently for all usage incurred across all sub-identities.
comparisons.iam_user: Not charged or paid independently; financial liability rolls up entirely to the master account.

advantages: Enables isolated credentials and minimum-privilege security scoping without multiple billing streams.
prerequisites: Master account must be logged in and fully authenticated.
best_practices: Assign permissions to a user group rather than directly to an isolated individual user.
Select region-specific scopes rather than all resources when minimum authorization requirements dictate constraint.
exam_traps: Assuming IAM users have separate credit lines or invoices distinct from the root account.
Expecting authorization policies to take effect instantaneously when they require 15 to 30 minutes.
common_confusions: Confusing Tenant Administrator (which excludes direct IAM management) with full account root administrative privileges.
real_world_scenarios: Creating a specific operations account for an external contractor limited purely to the AP-Singapore region.

sections.title: Creating and Configuring a VPC
learning_objectives: Provision a custom Virtual Private Cloud and associated subnet
Verify IAM user deployment rights within designated project scopes
Execute proper teardown procedures for networking resources
key_concepts: Subnet grouping dependencies during teardown operations
prerequisites: IAM user must be member of a group with sufficient network configuration policies in the region.
limitations: A VPC cannot be deleted directly if it contains active subnets.
best_practices: Delete the subnet information prior to initiating a VPC deletion request.
exam_traps: Attempting to delete a target VPC while the active subnet count remains greater than zero.
real_world_scenarios: Setting up the baseline localized isolation network framework in AP-Singapore for subsequent computing tier deployment.

sections.title: Introduction & Topology
learning_objectives: Identify capabilities of Elastic Cloud Server (ECS), Image Management Service (IMS), and Auto Scaling (AS)
Interpret the interactions between networking resources, private image definitions, and automated scaling parameters

definitions.definition: Provides scalable, on-demand computing cloud servers for secure, flexible, and efficient applications.
Enables full-lifecycle management for images, templates used to create servers or disks, helping quickly deploy services.
Automatically adjusts ECS instances based on service requirements and configured AS policies to adapt to fluctuating loads.

advantages: AS prevents unnecessary cloud charges while maintaining structural stability under heavy system load variations.

sections.title: ECS Lifecycle Management
learning_objectives: Provision pay-per-use Linux ECS instances inside custom subnets
Access Linux ECS servers via management console console options without public IPs
Modify computing specification flavors safely on stop-state resources

commands.purpose: Verifies the current configured memory space visible inside the operating system.

numbers.context: Standard system disk storage boundary specified during the public image template selection.

advantages: Specification changes allow instant processing power updates without data loss on system and data storage drives.
disadvantages: Modifying hardware resource allocation configurations causes momentary operational downtime during the adjustment cycle.
prerequisites: An active, configured Virtual Private Cloud infrastructure topology.
limitations: Direct network tools like PuTTY or CloudShell-based logging modes fail if the target machine lacks an assigned EIP or if relevant ports are blocked by the security group rules.
best_practices: Ensure the instance status reads 'Stopped' fully before initiating flavor re-sizing adjustments.
Utilize console direct login methods when checking unassigned EIP instances.
exam_traps: Attempting to adjust CPU or memory flavor specifications while the targeted cloud server remains active in the 'Running' state.
common_confusions: Confusing 'Stopped' state with full deletion or decommissioning of standard storage components.
real_world_scenarios: Scaling an application node's memory allocation capacity from 4GB up to 8GB after experiencing high utilization patterns.

sections.title: Creating a Linux System Disk Image from an ECS
learning_objectives: Configure critical platform guest utilities like Cloud-Init and Cloud ResetPwdAgent
Generate localized private images using configured cloud nodes
Execute in-region image replication and manage multi-tenant share operations

definitions.definition: A private image format targeting the primary bootable volume containing the fundamental operating ecosystem layout and pre-configured dependencies.

commands.purpose: Checks for the active presence of the required local cloud reset password agent binaries.
Queries system packages to check if the foundational customization management system utility is installed.
Locates the exact filesystem execution entry path for the cloud initialization script tool.

commands.command: systemctl enable cloud-init-local.service cloud-init.service cloud-config.service cloud-final.service
commands.purpose: Ensures cloud customization modules execute correctly automatically during subsequent node generation start sequences.
Inspects persistent hardware designation databases to protect against card enumeration mismatch issues.

numbers.context: Average operational completion time window observed during new image generation sequences.
Maximum concurrent recipient constraint cap imposed during single resource share operations.

advantages: Private imagery dramatically decreases software setup overhead across massive corporate system rollouts.
prerequisites: Target systems must reside in either standard Running or Stopped lifecycle conditions.
Internet breakout availability (via EIP) if performing forced cloud-init environment downloads manually.
limitations: Cross-region image sharing demands a regional copy function execution prior to targeting separate destinations.
Missing password reset agents isolate new target compute instances from accepting administrative updates.
best_practices: Verify network rule configurations are fully cleared out to stop potential interfaces designation adjustments.
Match target boot environments strictly (BIOS vs UEFI) between source nodes and modified templates.
exam_traps: Attempting an across-region direct template share using an unmatched region Project ID sequence.
Omitting network rule directory cleanup operations, leading to downstream network card attachment errors.
common_confusions: Conflating system disk images with full cloud node backups or multi-drive cluster groupings.
real_world_scenarios: Packaging a specialized corporate web environment template for distribution to secondary development teams located globally.

sections.title: Auto Scaling Operations
learning_objectives: Construct complex AS configurations matching active reference templates
Establish multi-AZ expansion structures containing scheduled and periodic policies
Configure dynamic BGP public network address bandwidth-responsive policies

definitions.definition: The structural blueprint defining instance specifications, image references, volumes, and credential types used during automated deployment actions.
An administrative cluster bounded by resource constraint rules containing instances subjected to matching automated operational policies.

numbers.context: Standard cooldown timeframe parameter used between periodic scheduling operations.
Standard delay duration configured for dynamic public network bandwidth adjustments.
Default configuration rule window representing the scaling health check grace period.

comparisons.load_balanced: Distributes deployed server instances equally across all configured availability zones.
comparisons.sequenced: Deploys instances down through a predetermined availability zone ordering list until reaching maximum boundaries.

advantages: Maintains high operational uptime while optimizing system expenditures by dynamically mapping node count to load rules.
prerequisites: An operational configuration baseline instance or reference system image template definition.
limitations: Bandwidth scaling mechanisms function exclusively on flexible pay-per-use public interface configurations.
best_practices: Disable standard AS actions before proceeding with intentional manual downstream environment deletions.
Incorporate multi-AZ configuration paths to insulate application deployments from independent data center events.
exam_traps: Forgetting to switch off or suspend an active AS Group before attempting infrastructure decommissioning, which triggers automated scaling actions.
Expecting a locked yearly-monthly public connection contract path to dynamically respond to dynamic bandwidth scaling rules.
common_confusions: Confusing the roles of an AS Configuration (the template blueprint) with an AS Group (the policy control boundaries).
real_world_scenarios: Automatically expanding application server pools at 18:00 every evening to absorb predictable high traffic shifts.
Dynamically boosting public pipeline allocation capacity up to 10 Mbit/s during scheduled marketing flash sales.

sections.title: Introduction & Topologies
learning_objectives: Differentiate isolated network capabilities spanning internal and cross-regional topologies
Identify the operational roles of internal group controls, public breakout endpoints, and path routing elements

definitions.definition: A logically isolated, configurable, and manageable virtual network for cloud servers, containers, and databases.
An access control system acting at the instance level that utilizes specific rules to filter inbound and outbound communication paths.
Static public network entry structures enabling internal platform components to negotiate communication paths with Internet endpoints.
An active system that distributes traffic across multiple backend targets based on user-defined listener rules to remove Single Points of Failure.
A direct regional routing layout enabling independent virtual private environments within a single geographic region to exchange traffic securely.
An encrypted, Internet-based communications tunnel connecting disparate distant network landscapes to local cloud instances.

limitations: VPC Peering pathways cannot cross separate geographical region definitions.

sections.title: VPC & ECS Core Infrastructure Provisioning
learning_objectives: Deploy distinct cloud spaces with non-overlapping network parameters across multiple global regions
Provision targeted server instances distributed down through independent local network subnets
limitations: IP address space mappings must remain completely non-conflicting when preparing environments for future cross-network setups.
best_practices: Isolate public frontend components from backend data endpoints by placing them in separate network subnets within the environment.
exam_traps: Configuring identical CIDR blocks on independent cloud networks intended for immediate interconnected communication layouts.
real_world_scenarios: Setting up clean infrastructure segments in AP-Singapore and AF-Johannesburg to test upcoming cross-regional application communication configurations.

sections.title: Verifying Network Functions & Connectivity Controls
learning_objectives: Verify internal private communication behaviors inside single cloud networks and isolate cross-VPC boundaries
Manipulate inbound access rule sets to demonstrate active traffic filtering controls
Implement public endpoint breakouts and deploy distributed high-availability application pools via load balancers

commands.purpose: Tests low-level ICMP pathway capabilities between distinct computing endpoints.
Launches a background HTTP listener service bound to port 8889 for traffic validation testing.
Tests the responsiveness of the local HTTP processing environment loopback interface.
Creates a uniquely identifiable trace indicator file on the host storage structure to verify load distribution paths.

numbers.context: The internal destination target port hosting backend computing node configurations inside this scenario.
The external consumer-facing entry point port specified on the frontend configuration of the load balancer listener.
The standard network interface delivery allocation parameter applied during public EIP environment allocation tests.

comparisons.same_vpc_different_subnets: Communication pathways are active and open out-of-the-box, assuming no blocking rules are present in the default security group settings.
comparisons.different_vpc_same_region: Communication is strictly blocked and unreachable unless an explicit interconnect component is provisioned.

advantages: Shared load balancing options efficiently balance user traffic flows while shielding underlying server systems from direct exposure to the open internet.
prerequisites: Compute nodes must be online, reachable, and running matching local validation script tools.
limitations: Total removal of all entry rules within a local security group completely blocks all incoming traffic paths, including standard ping commands from adjacent systems.
best_practices: Utilize precise cryptographic access systems (key pairs) when deploying publicly reachable server infrastructure platforms.
Verify backend target ports are properly exposed inside active firewall rule boundaries to ensure successful health check operations.
exam_traps: Expecting load balancers to correctly forward application traffic when the underlying security groups block all incoming communication paths from the balancer's source IP address range.
Assuming adjacent cloud networks can cross-communicate automatically without provisioning routing components or peering connections.
common_confusions: Confusing listener routing designations (frontend entry interfaces) with the actual back-end hosting application processing port configuration rules.
real_world_scenarios: Setting up a load-balanced public web application cluster in AP-Singapore that distributes HTTP requests across multiple independent internal server subnets.

prerequisites: Basic understanding of server operating systems (Linux)
Fundamental knowledge of core networking architectures and storage models
dependencies: Chapter 2 operations require the foundational IAM and VPC structures defined in Chapter 1.
Chapter 3 verification scenarios build directly upon the compute instances and systems created in Chapter 2.
